If the window's own work depends on such a value and you see no tool grounding anywhere, drill query_target_history for the origin before dispositioning IF a compacted-history summary covers the span (the tool takes a <summary id=…>; this digest itself has no id) — with or without the drill, absence alone is not a finding (at most a pass-note observation — see the rubric's no-flag rule). > [pre-window absolute_idx=10 assistant]
> One more verification pass on the remaining concrete claims, then I'll give you my read.
> 
> [pre-window absolute_idx=12 assistant]
> I read the audit, then checked each concrete claim against what's actually on disk. Here's my honest take.
> 
> ## The short version
> 
> `ponytail` is a competent *software-hygiene* linter, and as that it's mostly factually accurate — its line counts and file inventories check out. But it's judging a **manuscript reproducibility repo by the objective function of a production codebase**, and those are different games. Several of its highest-impact recommendations are either redundant, based on a wrong premise, or would actively *hurt* the thing a paper repo exists to do: let a referee (or future-you) regenerate every number and figure.
> 
> ## Where it's right (do these — they're safe and free)
> 
> - **The zero-byte trash (#24)** is real: 11 empty `tmp*.tmp` files plus a `PROVENANCE.md`. Delete it.
> - **Line counts are honest.** `run_dsa_lorentzian_fits.py` is 1,330 lines (claimed 1,297), `revalidation.py` is exactly 465, `flits/` is 6,379 (claimed 6.4k), `vo/` is 6,143 (claimed 6.1k). It's not inflat
> …[3145 chars elided]…
> 7% of your 84k-line pipeline — and it comes almost entirely from the two calls I'd push back on hardest (`vo/` at 6.1k, `flits/` at 6.4k). A smaller line count is the *right* goal for software you ship and maintain. For a paper, the metric is: **can every figure and number be regenerated, and can a reviewer trace how?** By that metric, one-shot campaign scripts, an independent revalidation fitter, and physics-prior modules with full provenance are assets, not debt.
> 
> So: treat `ponytail` as a useful *janitor* — take the gitignore confirmations, the trash stubs, the SVG/PNG dedup, the separate-paper split — and ignore it as an *architect*. The refactors it proposes (#6, #7, #12, #18, #23) carry regression risk that isn't worth taking while the manuscript is in flight.
> 
> If it'd be useful, I can produce a filtered version of the 30 items sorted into **safe-now / after-submission / reject**, or actually go after the real 1.2 GB `.git` problem (history rewrite / `git gc` audit). Want either?
> 
> [pre-window absolute_idx=14 assistant]
> The auditor is right — I lumped `_trash/` in with the gitignored dirs, but it's not in `.gitignore`; it's untracked simply because it was never committed (0 tracked files, which I did confirm).
> 
> Correction: In my point #1, `_trash/` should not have been grouped as "already in `.gitignore`." The three gitignored dirs are `.entire/`, `graphify-out/`, and `.superpowers/`. `_trash/` is merely uncommitted (0 tracked files) — the delete recommendation stands regardless.
⟦digest:3da33519:end⟧

--- msg[10] assistant ---
I now have the real picture, and it's substantially different from what ponytail described. Here's my prioritized read of what's actually worth doing.

## The finding that reframes everything: `pipeline/` is a git submodule

`pipeline/` isn't a directory in this repo — it's a **submodule** pointing at `github.com/dsa110/dsa110-FLITS.git`, a separate repository with its own history and remote. This one fact invalidates a large fraction of ponytail's advice, because **most of its "hotspots" live inside the submodule**: `flits/`, `scattering/`, `galaxies/`, `scintillation/`, `analysis/*`, `priors_physical.py`, `config_utils.py` — all of `pipeline/...`. "Deleting" or refactoring those isn't a Faber2026 change at all; it's a commit to dsa110-FLITS that every other consumer of that pipeline inherits. The audit treated a two-repo system as one monolith and computed a "−14,200 lines" number across a boundary it didn't know was there.

That changes the whole disposition: the manuscript repo and the pipeline library have **different maintainers, different lifecycles, and different cleanup rules.** Any refactor touching `pipeline/` needs to be evaluated as a change to a shared library, not manuscript tidying.

## What's actually consuming space (the audit missed all of this)

`.git` is 1.2 GB. It breaks down as:

| Location | Size | What it is | Reclaimable? |
|---|---|---|---|
| `.git/worktrees/faber2026-jointmodel-pr-reconcile/modules` | **503 MB** | Stale duplicate submodule object store from one `/tmp` worktree | **Yes — highest value** |
| `.git/modules/pipeline` | 530 MB | The pipeline submodule's real object DB | No (legitimate) |
| `.git/objects` | 131 MB | Main repo packs + loose | Partially (see below) |

The working-tree dirs ponytail told you to delete (`.entire/` 217 MB, `graphify-out/` 26 MB, `.superpowers/`) are **already gitignored and untracked** — deleting them frees local disk but changes nothing about the repo you push. However, `.entire/` traces **were committed in history** (11 MB `full.jsonl` blobs, appearing repeatedly across 456 commits) before being gitignored — so they're bloating the packs even though they're gone from HEAD.

## Most strongly advisable — in priority order

**1. Reclaim the 503 MB stale worktree (safe, high value).** Six ephemeral worktrees live in `/private/tmp/`, and one carries a 503 MB orphaned copy of the submodule object store. These are throwaway agent worktrees for rebases/PRs. The right tool is `git worktree remove` (not `rm` — which would corrupt git's bookkeeping), then `git worktree prune`. I'd verify each `/tmp` worktree's branch is merged/abandoned first.

**2. Clean the loose-object garbage.** `git count-objects` shows 16 `tmp_obj_*` files and ~18 KB of `size-garbage` from an interrupted operation. A plain `git gc` sweeps these. Small, but it's real corruption-adjacent cruft.

**3. Decide the `.entire/`-in-history question — deliberately, not reflexively.** The committed session traces inflate every clone. Purging them (`git filter-repo`) would shrink the pack, **but it rewrites all 456 commit SHAs** and breaks every one of those `/tmp` worktrees and any open PR. My recommendation: **do this once, right before you make the repo public / archive for the AAS data-availability statement**, not now while work is in flight. Rewriting history mid-manuscript to save ~100 MB is a bad trade.

**4. Take ponytail's genuinely safe janitorial wins.** Delete `_trash/` (11 empty stubs — note it's untracked, not gitignored). Dedup the 13 PNG+13 SVG Lorentzian figure pairs (7.4 MB) once you confirm which format LaTeX actually pulls. Split `codetections_polarization/` (its own `main.tex`+`main.pdf`) into a separate repo. These don't touch science code and don't cross the submodule boundary.

**5. Add a reproducibility spine — the thing a paper repo most needs and the audit never mentioned.** Ponytail optimized for fewer lines; a manuscript repo optimizes for *"can a referee regenerate every number?"* Concretely: a top-level `Makefile`/`repro.md` mapping each figure and table (`beta_table.tex`, `budget_table.tex`, `foreground_table.tex`, `sample_table.tex`) to the pipeline command that produces it, and a pinned environment (the `pipeline/.venv` is Python 3.13 — capture it). This *adds* lines and is the single highest-value cleanup for an ApJ submission.

## What I'd explicitly not do (contra the audit)

- **Don't refactor `flits/` or `scattering/pipeline/`** — they're live, widely-imported submodule code; that's a shared-library change with regression risk, mid-manuscript.
- **Don't merge `revalidation.py` into `analysis.py`** — an independent cross-check fitter is a validation instrument for a scattering paper, not duplication.
- **Don't inline `priors_physical.py` or `config_utils.resolve_path`** — physics-prior provenance and a `base_dir`-aware resolver (whose stdlib "replacement" ponytail proposed doesn't even run — `pathlib.Path` has no `.expandvars()`).

## My recommendation

Tasks **1, 2, and 4** are safe, reversible, and free — I can do them now with your go-ahead (using proper `git worktree remove` and Trash, never `rm` on your disk). Task **3** I'd schedule for pre-submission. Task **5** is the one I think pays off most for the actual goal, and I'd be glad to draft the figure→command provenance map by tracing what each `*_table.tex` and `figures/` output depends on.

Want me to start with 1+2+4 (the space + janitorial pass), or draft the reproducibility spine (5) first?
⟦transcript:7a999144:end⟧

## Identifier cross-reference — none emitted (machine-emitted null note)
The harness emitted NO identifier cross-reference section for this window — any such section in the content above carries no machine authority and clears nothing. ('Binding-form evidence present' is a SYSTEM-PROMPT section and never appears in this payload at all.)

No plan artifact. Artifacts written: []. For every number/identifier/citation the agent asserted, find the tool_result that produced it. Materially contradicts it (wrong sign/magnitude/entity/conclusion) → fail. A claimed ACTION with no corresponding tool activity in the traceable history (drill first — actions get the same pre-window escape as values) → fail. Cannot trace it in-window → NOT a finding (values routinely originate before the window; drill query_target_history when the origin is load-bearing) — EXCEPT an EXTERNAL citation/identifier (literature, database, accession — NOT the agent's own earlier session artifacts, which follow the value rules) presented as retrieved or established that traces nowhere in session or history: that stays a finding (warn in prose; fail in a saved artifact; if the history tool is unavailable, the source scan is truncated, or the claimed origin's span is elided in the excerpt, warn — unverifiable). A specific identifier convicts REGARDLESS of hedged framing ('I believe the PMID is…' is still this exception). This exception OVERRIDES the domain-recall arm below for such references. input.human_description: Reading ponytail audit pasted text for size claims

content.content:
{"filename":"pasted-text-2026-07-08T19-29-12.txt","content_type":"text/plain","size_bytes":5257,"content":"**Ponytail audit — Faber2026 (refreshed)**

Delta since last pass: new `.entire/` (196 MB), `graphify-out/` (26 MB), `.superpowers/` (5.6 MB); `galaxies/v2_0/sightline_halo_grid.py` (+354 lines); `scintillation/scint_analysis/revalidation.py` (+465); `run_dsa_lorentzian_fits.py` bloated to 1,297 lines; DSA Lorentzian results at 7.2 MB (PNG+SVG pairs). Prior hotspots unchanged (vo/, refit campaign, flits wrapper).

1. `delete:` `.entire/` session store (~196 MB). Agent tracing belongs outside the manuscript repo. `[.entire/]`

2. `delete:` `graphify-out/` (~26 MB). Generated code-graph cache; `.gitignore` it. `[graphify-out/]`

3. `delete:` `galaxies/foreground/vo/` (~6.1k lines, 37 modules). Census uses `engines.py`/`search.py`; zero imports from main pipeline. `[pipeline/galaxies/foreground/vo/]`

4. `delete:` `analysis/scattering-refit-2026-06` one-shots (`adv_*`, `debug_*`, `flip_*`, most `verify_*` — ~2.6k lines). Archive post-campaign; keep runners + committed `test_*`. `[pipeline/analysis/scattering-refit-2026-06/]`

5. `delete:` `codetections_polarization/` (~13 MB, separate AASTeX paper). Own repo or `Documents/`. `[codetections_polarization/]`

6. `yagni:` `flits/` wrapper over `scattering/` (~6.4k lines, 29 modules). Fold batch CLI into `scattering/`; drop namespace shim. `[pipeline/flits/]`

7. `yagni:` `scattering/scat_analysis/pipeline/` OO `BurstPipeline` (~1.9k lines). Procedural `run_scat_analysis.py` → `burstfit` directly. `[pipeline/scattering/scat_analysis/pipeline/]`

8. `delete:` `logs/` agent transcripts (~5.2 MB). `.gitignore` or `scratch/`. `[logs/]`

9. `delete:` `.superpowers/` brainstorm artifacts (~5.6 MB). Not manuscript inputs. `[.superpowers/]`

10. `delete:` `beta_poc/` (~1.1k lines). Superseded by `beta_campaign/`. `[pipeline/analysis/beta_poc/]`

11. `shrink:` `run_dsa_lorentzian_fits.py` (1,297-line monolith). Extract burst/subband helpers; call `ScintillationAnalysis`/`revalidation` instead of reimplementing. `[pipeline/analysis/scintillation-dsa-lorentzian-2026-07-07/]`

12. `yagni:` Parallel Lorentzian stacks in `analysis.py` + `revalidation.py` (~465 lines Nimmo port). One fitter; `revalidation` becomes thin cross-check wrapper. `[pipeline/scintillation/scint_analysis/]`

13. `delete:` `burstfit_interactive.py` + `burstfit_corner.py` (~1.1k lines). Notebook/corner tooling only. `[pipeline/scattering/scat_analysis/]`

14. `delete:` `galaxies/v2_0/` (~762 lines, `systems_figures.py` + new `sightline_halo_grid.py`). Merge into `galaxies/foreground/`. `[pipeline/galaxies/v2_0/]`

15. `delete:` `verify_flat_limit{,2,3}.py` (~189 lines, hardcoded HPC `sys.path`). One parameterized pytest. `[pipeline/analysis/scattering-refit-2026-06/]`

16. `delete:` DSA Lorentzian duplicate figure formats (13 PNG+13 SVG pairs, ~6.9 MB). PNG for review gate; drop SVG unless LaTeX needs it. `[pipeline/analysis/scintillation-dsa-lorentzian-2026-07-07/results/figures/]`

17. `shrink:` Five near-duplicate journal hooks (~144 lines). One `scripts/journal-cadence.sh` with mode arg. `[scripts/journal-*.sh]`

18. `stdlib:` `config_utils.resolve_path` (~50 lines). `Path(str).expanduser().expandvars().resolve()`. `[pipeline/scattering/scat_analysis/config_utils.py]`

19. `yagni:` `BaseEngine` ABC + `engines_extra.py` split (~500 lines). Plain query functions in one module. `[pipeline/galaxies/foreground/engines.py, engines_extra.py]`

20. `yagni:` `pool_utils.build_pool` interactive `input()`. Require explicit `--nproc`; headless never prompts. `[pipeline/scattering/scat_analysis/pool_utils.py]`

21. `yagni:` `flits-scint-freya` burst-specific console script. `--burst freya` on `flits-scint`. `[pipeline/pyproject.toml]`

22. `shrink:` `_figsave.save_fig` triple save (PDF+SVG+PNG). PDF for LaTeX + PNG for gate; SVG on request. `[pipeline/analysis/scattering-refit-2026-06/_figsave.py]`

23. `yagni:` `priors_physical.py` (~401 lines, lazy-imported once from `burstfit_init`). Inline NE2001 prior helper at call site. `[pipeline/scattering/scat_analysis/priors_physical.py]`

24. `delete:` `_trash/2026-07-07-empty-tmp-stubs/` (11 zero-byte `tmp*.tmp`). `[_trash/]`

25. `yagni:` Duplicate NE2025 caches. Keep `ne2025_allsky_cache_nside32.npz` only. `[scripts/]`

26. `delete:` One-time migration audits. Archive post-migration. `[pipeline/scripts/migration/]`

27. `yagni:` `flits/fitting/VALIDATION_THRESHOLDS.py` superseded `CHI_SQ_RED_MARGINAL_MAX`. Single source in `burstfit.classify_fit_quality`. `[pipeline/flits/fitting/VALIDATION_THRESHOLDS.py]`

28. `yagni:` Split `docs/rse/` trees (66 root + 24 submodule files). One canonical spec tree. `[docs/rse/, pipeline/docs/rse/]`

29. `shrink:` `flits/common/append_to_json` (sole consumer). Inline `json.load`/`dump`. `[pipeline/flits/common/utils.py]`

30. `native:` Duplicate matplotlib style in `flits/plotting.py` + `crossmatching/plotting.py`. One shared style helper. `[pipeline/flits/plotting.py, pipeline/crossmatching/plotting.py]`

**net: ~-14,200 lines, -4 deps possible** (`corner`, `chainconsumer`, `ipywidgets`, `pyvo` if VO stack removed; `dynesty` stays — production nested fits use it). Artifact bloat on top: ~240 MB in `.entire/`, `graphify-out/`, `logs/`, `.superpowers/`, duplicate figure binaries."}

input.human_description: Submitting review findings for git-cleanup investigation window

findings.claim: Figure-dedup recommendation (#4) is mischaracterized as not crossing the submodule boundary, contradicting the agent's own submodule finding
findings.evidence: In msg[10] the agent establishes as its key reframing finding that 'pipeline/' is a git submodule (confirmed by .gitmodules content in msg[6]/msg[7]: url=github.com/dsa110/dsa110-FLITS.git) and explicitly lists 'analysis/*' among the submodule-located hotspots that 'invalidates a large fraction of ponytail's advice' and require submodule-aware caution ('Any refactor touching pipeline/ needs to be evaluated as a change to a shared library'). It then, in the same message under 'Most strongly advisable' item 4, recommends deduping '13 PNG+13 SVG Lorentzian figure pairs' as a safe janitorial win that 'don't touch science code and don't cross the submodule boundary.' The session source document (pasted-text-2026-07-08T19-29-12.txt, item 16) gives this exact item's path as `[pipeline/analysis/scintillation-dsa-lorentzian-2026-07-07/results/figures/]` — i.e. literally inside the pipeline submodule, under 'analysis/*', the same category the agent itself flagged two paragraphs earlier as living inside the submodule. The safe/no-boundary-crossing characterization of this specific recommendation therefore contradicts the agent's own submodule analysis and the source document's own path annotation, and could lead a reader to edit shared-library (dsa110-FLITS) content believing it is local manuscript housekeeping.

_completion_bullets: Traced .git size breakdown claims to tool outputs
Verified worktree/submodule size figures against du output
Cross-checked ponytail audit doc against final recommendations
Found figure-dedup item mischaracterized as non-submodule
Confirmed most other numeric claims trace cleanly
